text: I find myself in a modern, almost sterile lobby leading into a room devoted to R+D—Research and Development. The glass, the neon glow, the crisp clarity of information everywhere: it all has an air of ordered curiosity. But as I enter the space and read the texts displayed, I notice a silent tension, a feeling of being on the edge of something innovative but not quite graspable yet. The environment invites analysis and reflection, but it also feels detached—like I am both observer and subject in some unfolding experiment. I feel curious, slightly unsettled, and eager to see what questions or insights might emerge from this artificial calm.
